detaileddescription.para: Datalog stream ID for motor controller telemetry.

memberdef.name: SYN_MOTOR_CTRL_DEFAULTS
memberdef.initializer: ((SYN_MotorCtrl_Config){ \ .motor = (motor_out), \ .read_pos = (read_fn), \ .read_pos_ctx = (read_ctx), \ .update_hz = (hz), \ .output_min = -(out_max), \ .output_max = (out_max), \ .pid_kp = 1 << 6, /* 0.25 effective (conservative) */ \ .pid_ki = 0, /* no integral by default */ \ .pid_kd = 0, /* no derivative by default */ \ .pid_scale = 8, /* gains ÷ 256 */ \ .position_deadband = 2, \ .stall_timeout_ms = 1000, \ .stall_threshold = 1, \ })

briefdescription.para: Convenience macro for a motor controller config with sane defaults.

detaileddescription.para: Provides a conservative P-only configuration that is stable on most systems. Override individual fields after initialization to tune.
Motor stopped, controller off
Maintain target velocity
Move to target position
Direct output, no PID feedback
Motor off, controller idle
Actively driving toward target
Position mode: within deadband
Feedback not changing despite output
Hit a soft position limit
Returns the current position in whatever units the sensor provides (encoder counts, ADC ticks, millivolts — the PID doesn't care). The same units must be used for all targets and deadbands.

briefdescription.para: Callback invoked when a motor stall is detected.
Callback invoked when position reaches the target deadband.
Drive the motor at a fixed output level (open-loop).

detaileddescription.para: Bypasses PID — the output value is passed directly to the motor. Useful for manual jogging, testing motor wiring, or when simple open-loop control is sufficient. Position feedback is still read (for soft limits and stall detection) but not used for control.
Switches to velocity mode. Positive = forward, negative = reverse.

parameterdescription.para: Target velocity in feedback units per second.

detaileddescription.para: Switches to position mode. Controller drives toward target and enters ON_TARGET state when within deadband.

briefdescription.para: Feed a trajectory point for feedforward + PID tracking.

detaileddescription.para: Call this each update with the current output of your trajectory generator (syn_ramp, syn_scurve, or application code). The controller uses PID on position error plus feedforward from the trajectory's velocity and acceleration.

memberdef.argsstring: (SYN_MotorCtrl *ctrl, int32_t target, int32_t max_velocity, int32_t acceleration)
memberdef.name: syn_motor_ctrl_move_to

briefdescription.para: Move to position with a built-in trapezoidal velocity profile.

detaileddescription.para: This is the "batteries included" path — no external profile generator needed. The controller internally generates a trapezoidal ramp (accelerate → cruise → decelerate) and feeds it through the normal trajectory/feedforward path each update() call.
Equivalent to manually calling syn_ramp_set_target_trapezoid() and feeding the output into set_trajectory() each tick, but with zero user-side wiring.

memberdef.argsstring: (SYN_MotorCtrl *ctrl, int32_t target, int32_t max_velocity, int32_t max_accel, int32_t max_jerk)
memberdef.name: syn_motor_ctrl_move_to_scurve

briefdescription.para: Move to position with a built-in jerk-limited S-curve profile.

detaileddescription.para: Like move_to(), but uses a 7-phase S-curve trajectory that bounds jerk for the smoothest possible motion. The S-curve natively provides position, velocity, and acceleration — all three are fed into the trajectory/feedforward path.

All kinematic limits are in per-second units:
max_velocity: units/second
max_accel: units/second²
max_jerk: units/second³

briefdescription.para: Stop the motor and enter idle mode.
Run the control loop. Call at cfg.update_hz rate.

detaileddescription.para: Reads feedback, computes PID, drives motor output. If a datalog is attached, writes a SYN_MotorCtrl_Sample each call.

parameterdescription.para: Callback function, or NULL to unregister.
Callback function, or NULL to unregister.

memberdef.argsstring: (SYN_MotorCtrl *ctrl, int32_t kp, int32_t ki, int32_t kd)
memberdef.name: syn_motor_ctrl_set_gains

briefdescription.para: Clear a stall condition and return to idle.

detaileddescription.para: After a stall, the controller locks in STALLED state until this is called.

briefdescription.para: Attach a datalog for tuning capture.

detaileddescription.para: When attached, every update() writes a SYN_MotorCtrl_Sample frame to the datalog at full control-loop rate.

parameterdescription.para: Datalog instance (caller-owned), or NULL to detach.

briefdescription.para: Reset move metrics. Call before starting a move you want to measure.

detaileddescription.para: Read this after a move completes to evaluate performance. Contains max error, RMS error data, overshoot, peak output, and timing — everything needed for tuning, in a single read.

briefdescription.para: Compute RMS tracking error from accumulated metrics.

detaileddescription.para: Returns the integer square root of (error_sq_sum / sample_count). Only valid if sample_count > 0.

simplesect.para: RMS tracking error in feedback units.
Duration in ms, or 0 if no move started.

briefdescription.para: Get settling time in milliseconds (0 if never settled).
Closed-loop motor controller — generic feedback + PID + motor.

detaileddescription.para:
Combines any position/velocity feedback source with PID control to drive DC or stepper motors in closed loop. The feedback source is a function pointer, so it works equally well with:
Rotary encoder (syn_encoder)
Potentiometer / linear pot on ADC (syn_adc)
Hall sensor, absolute encoder, or any other position source

listitem.para: Position: PID drives to target position value
Trajectory: PID + feedforward tracks a trajectory from an external generator (syn_ramp, syn_scurve, or application code)
